content:
# elizaos-plugins/registry Daily Update (Mar 29, 2026)
## OVERVIEW 
The day saw a new plugin proposal for spend governance and a new pull request to add a plugin to the registry. Discussions continued on the critical need for autonomous agent spend governance.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS

No completed work to report for this period.

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
- A new feature pull request was opened to add the `@axnetfun/plugin-axnet` to the registry. This PR is currently open and awaiting review. [#324](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/pull/324)

## CLOSED ISSUES

No closed issues to report for this period.

## NEW ISSUES

No new issues to report for this period.

## ACTIVE ISSUES

**Autonomous Agent Spend Governance**
Discussion continued on the critical need for a plugin to manage autonomous agent spend governance, as highlighted in [#6695](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/issues/6695). The latest comments emphasize the distinction between policy-based checks (e.g., Dreamline) and explicit human operator authorization for specific payments. A proposed three-object contract (`payment_required`, `payment_approval`, `payment_receipt`) was outlined to enable a pre-authorization layer, ensuring human oversight before x402 payments execute. The discussion also raised a question for the ElizaOS team regarding whether the current x402 plugin surfaces any pre-execution events to the operator.
